Extract all indicators of compromise from this MDR security report:

Investigative Summary
---------------------
At 2025-03-17 13:30:01 UTC, Huntress observed user "HarrisonDent" executing a malicious process WScript.exe with the following command line arguments "C:\Windows\System32\WScript.exe" "C:\Users\hdent\Downloads\Chrome_231.23.85505.js". After execution, Hidden NetSupport was installed in the directory "C:\ProgramData\n88aelgk\". Please follow the remediation steps below:

Gootloader / SocGholish: Malware like Gootloader and SocGholish can download additional malicious tooling, provision remote access to a threat actor, and deploy persistence to stay on the host. This malware's initial access typically comes from user-interaction from web browsing and social engineering emails. 

Hidden NetSupport: NetSupport is a legitimate remote management tool that is being misused by a threat actor to gain unauthorized access and control of the reported endpoint. Threat actors do this to hide their presence, maintain persistence and further exploit the network.

Remediations:
-------------
Assisted Remediations provided by the Huntress SOC to remediate the incident. These can be executed automatically in the Huntress Platform:
- Kill Process - path: C:\ProgramData\n88aelgk\client32.exe + pid: 42844
- Kill Process - path: C:\Windows\System32\WScript.exe + pid: 44344
- Delete Registry Value - key: HKU\S-1-12-1-3790649404-1198326543-187141521-2282935963\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run + value: MyHelp
- Kill Process - path: C:\ProgramData\n88aelgk\client32.exe + pid: 42844

- Delete File - path: C:\ProgramData\n88aelgk\\AudioCapture.dll

- + 27 more

Manual Remediations provided by the Huntress SOC are highly recommended remediation actions to be conducted by your team before resolving the incident in the Huntress Platform:

- Audit affected directories/files for additional suspicious files and remove those found. 

All remediations provided can be found in the Huntress Platfrom: Incident Report: https://tabinc.huntress.io/org/105275/infection_reports/1449499#remediations-tab

Investigative Summary
---------------------
At 2025-04-15 18:43:39 UTC, Huntress observed user "Staples / S-1-5-21-232964252-859390665-2156229966-1540" executing anomalous domain/system enumeration for the host "ARDENTRDS". This user was first seen authenticating via RDS with the IP address "178.162.222[.]163" and workstation name "LAPTOP-LVV1JFIM" at "2025-04-15T15:50:14", which the Huntress agent is not installed on. After authentication, the user was also seen executing the programs "C:\Users\staples\Desktop\pia-v82-installer-win.exe, C:\Users\staples\Desktop\xDedicRDPClient.exe". These files were not recoverable at the time of the investigation. Please follow the remediation steps below. 

Enumeration (Active directory, Network, Cloud, etc): Enumeration allows a threat actor to identity users, groups, privileges, and assets that can facilitate further ingress and compromise. 

Persistence : Persistence consists of techniques that adversaries use to keep access to systems across restarts, changed credentials, and other interruptions that could cut off their access. Techniques used for persistence include any access, action, or configuration changes that let them maintain their foothold on systems, such as replacing or hijacking legitimate code or adding startup code.

Remediations:
-------------
Assisted Remediations provided by the Huntress SOC to remediate the incident. These can be executed automatically in the Huntress Platform:
